meanings.definition: indicate a place, direction, person, or thing; either spatially or figuratively
examples: I showed the customer the glove section
He pointed to the empty parking space
he indicated his opponents
The weather vane points North
the dancers toes pointed outward

meanings.definition: direct into a position for use
examples: point a gun
He charged his weapon at me

meanings.definition: direct the course; determine the direction of travelling
be a signal for or a symptom of
examples: These symptoms indicate a serious illness
Her behavior points to a severe neurosis
The economic indicators signal that the euro is undervalued
point the letter

meanings.definition: mark (a psalm text) to indicate the points at which the music changes
be positionable in a specified manner
examples: The gun points with ease

meanings.definition: intend (something) to move towards a certain goal
examples: He aimed his fists towards his opponent's face
criticism directed at her superior
direct your anger towards others, not towards yourself

meanings.definition: indicate the presence of (game) by standing and pointing with the muzzle
examples: the dog pointed the dead duck
The candles are tapered
point a chimney

meanings.definition: direct and obvious in meaning or reference; often unpleasant
examples: a pointed critique
a pointed allusion to what was going on
another pointed look in their direction
